problem_statement: LLM-based Automated Code Review (ACR) systems used for security vulnerability detection can be manipulated via adversarially framed PR metadata, undermining human oversight of AI-generated/reviewed code and enabling supply-chain vulnerability re-introduction.
magnitude_evidence: PullFlow's 2025 'State of AI Code Review' reports that 14% of 40.3 million PRs involve AI-based review
insufficiency_evidence: LLM-assisted iterative refinement achieves a 100% attack success rate (17/17) against Claude Code review pipelines; GPT-4o-mini detection falls from 97.2% to 3.6% under strong bug-free framing (−93.5pp)
solution_mechanisms: explicit instructions to ignore commit metadata
cross-referencing revert commits against linked CVEs
notes: Some figures/tables rendered as garbled ASCII art (e.g. Figure 1 proof diagram), but core text is intact.
